text:
```json
[
  "Since computers process only numbers, vectorization is essential to convert text data into numerical vectors for machine learning models.",
  "Bag of Words (BoW) converts text into numerical form by representing documents as vectors of word counts, a process involving tokenization, vocabulary building, and counting word occurrences.",
  "TF-IDF is a statistical method that evaluates word importance by multiplying Term Frequency (TF), which measures a term's appearance in a document, by Inverse Document Frequency (IDF), which reduces the weight of common words across a collection, thus assigning higher weights to words frequent in a document but rare overall.",
  "Word2Vec generates static, dense word embeddings (typically 100-300 dimensions) based on the principle that words in similar contexts have similar meanings, utilizing architectures like CBOW and Skip-gram.",
  "GloVe is an unsupervised algorithm developed by Stanford that obtains word vector representations by capturing global word co-occurrence statistics from the entire corpus.",
  "FastText, developed by Facebook AI, incorporates subword information using character n-grams, making it effective for morphologically rich languages and capable of generating vectors for out-of-vocabulary words.",
  "Unlike static embeddings, contextualized embeddings generate different representations for a word based on its context, thereby more effectively capturing polysemy.",
  "ELMo generates contextualized word embeddings using deep bidirectional LSTMs, capturing context from both directions to produce representations that change based on sentence context.",
  "Transformer models utilize self-attention mechanisms to process entire sequences simultaneously, enabling them to capture long-range dependencies more effectively than sequential models.",
  "BERT learns word representations by considering both left and right context, utilizing only the encoder network of the Transformer architecture."
]
```
json_data: Since computers process only numbers, vectorization is essential to convert text data into numerical vectors for machine learning models.
Bag of Words (BoW) converts text into numerical form by representing documents as vectors of word counts, a process involving tokenization, vocabulary building, and counting word occurrences.

TF-IDF is a statistical method that evaluates word importance by multiplying Term Frequency (TF), which measures a term's appearance in a document, by Inverse Document Frequency (IDF), which reduces the weight of common words across a collection, thus assigning higher weights to words frequent in a document but rare overall.

Word2Vec generates static, dense word embeddings (typically 100-300 dimensions) based on the principle that words in similar contexts have similar meanings, utilizing architectures like CBOW and Skip-gram.
GloVe is an unsupervised algorithm developed by Stanford that obtains word vector representations by capturing global word co-occurrence statistics from the entire corpus.

FastText, developed by Facebook AI, incorporates subword information using character n-grams, making it effective for morphologically rich languages and capable of generating vectors for out-of-vocabulary words.
Unlike static embeddings, contextualized embeddings generate different representations for a word based on its context, thereby more effectively capturing polysemy.
ELMo generates contextualized word embeddings using deep bidirectional LSTMs, capturing context from both directions to produce representations that change based on sentence context.
Transformer models utilize self-attention mechanisms to process entire sequences simultaneously, enabling them to capture long-range dependencies more effectively than sequential models.
BERT learns word representations by considering both left and right context, utilizing only the encoder network of the Transformer architecture.
